name: Tour around the Alloz Reservoir from Estella-Lizarra via Eraul's Holm Oak
name_original: Vuelta al Embalse de Alloz desde Estella-Lizarra por la Encina de Eraul
region_intro: Road cycling around Estella/Lizarra offers diverse landscapes, transitioning from mountainous terrain to riverside environments. The region is characterized by natural parks like Urbasa-Andia, featuring karst massifs and extensive beech forests, alongside the calm waters of the Alloz Reservoir. Cyclists encounter varied elevation gains, from challenging climbs in the sierras to more moderate routes along river valleys such as the Ega. This blend of natural features provides a range of road cycling experiences for different skill levels. Best road cycling routes around Estella/Lizarra The most popular road cycling route is Tour of the Ports of the Urbasa-Andia Natural Park, a 56.5 miles (91.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 34 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Tour around the Alloz Reservoir from Estella-Lizarra via Eraul's Holm Oak, a moderate 28.1 miles (45.3 km) path. This route offers scenic views of the calm, turquoise waters of the reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Plaza de San Martín – Acedo Campsite loop from Estella-Lizarra, a 51.3 miles (82.6 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Road cycling around Estella/Lizarra is defined by natural parks, reservoirs, and mountain ranges like Sierra de Lokiz, offering routes for various ability levels. The routes in Estella/Lizarra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Estella/Lizarra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Estella/Lizarra?
A: There are over 60 road cycling routes around Estella/Lizarra, offering a wide range of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 10 reviews.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Estella/Lizarra?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, transitioning from mountainous areas to riverside environments. You'll find challenging climbs in natural parks like Urbasa-Andia, featuring karst massifs and extensive beech forests, as well as more moderate routes along river valleys such as the Ega. The area also includes scenic loops around the Alloz Reservoir and routes through the Sierra de Lokiz.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, Estella/Lizarra offers several routes suitable for beginners or those preferring an easier ride. While many routes feature varied elevation, there are 3 easy routes and 34 moderate routes available. These often follow gentler gradients along river valleys or around the Alloz Reservoir, providing a less strenuous experience.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers 23 difficult routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984696378/tour-of-the-ports-of-the-urbasa-andia-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of the Ports of the Urbasa-Andia Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, a 91.0 km trail with significant elevation gain, ideal for testing your endurance through stunning natural landscapes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of significant natural features and landmarks. You can cycle past the calm, turquoise waters of the Alloz Reservoir, explore the extensive beech forests of the Urbasa-Andia Natural Park, or follow the scenic Ega River. Historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/718255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wine Fountain at Bodegas Irache&lt;/a&gt;, a unique stop on the Camino de Santiago, are also accessible on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20163288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de San Martín – Irache Wine Fountain loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots accessible by road bike?
A: Absolutely. The Urbasa-Andia Natural Park, a 'jewel' surrounding Estella-Lizarra, offers breathtaking panoramic views from various vantage points, such as the Ubaba viewpoint overlooking the Améscoa valley. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40379085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Lokiz – Estella-Lizarra loop&lt;/a&gt; also provide scenic vistas of the surrounding mountain ranges.

Q: What historical attractions can I visit while road cycling in the area?
A: Estella/Lizarra is rich in history, being an important stop on the Way of St. James. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/718255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wine Fountain at Bodegas Irache&lt;/a&gt;, located near the historic Monastery of Irache. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3745111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estella-Lizarra&lt;/a&gt; itself is an open-air museum with Romanesque churches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7387615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro de la Rúa&lt;/a&gt; and the Palace of the Kings of Navarre, providing a charming backdrop for your rides.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Estella/Lizarra?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Estella/Lizarra are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20163367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de San Martín – Acedo Campsite loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20163288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de San Martín – Irache Wine Fountain loop&lt;/a&gt;, offering convenient options for exploration.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Estella/Lizarra?
A: Estella/Lizarra benefits from a mild climate, shielded by surrounding mountains. While specific seasonal recommendations depend on personal preference, spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, making them ideal for road cycling. Summer can be warm, especially in the valleys, while winter might bring cooler temperatures, particularly at higher elevations in the natural parks.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Estella/Lizarra?
A: While road cycling routes often involve shared roads, the region's diverse terrain includes options that can be more suitable for families. Look for routes with less elevation gain or those that utilize quieter local roads. The Vasco-Navarro Railway Greenway, an old railway line, offers a scenic and generally flatter path that can be a good option for a more relaxed family ride, though it's not exclusively a road cycling route.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Estella/Lizarra?
A: Estella/Lizarra, as a central hub, offers various parking options within the town itself, which can serve as a starting point for many routes. For routes venturing into natural parks or specific attractions, it's advisable to check local signage for designated parking areas. Many routes begin and end in the town, making it convenient to park centrally.

Q: What makes Estella/Lizarra a recognized cycling destination?
A: Estella/Lizarra is gaining recognition as a cycling hub due to its diverse landscapes, strong local cycling culture (evidenced by the Club Ciclista Estella), and its strategic location on the Eurovelo 1 and 3 routes. This combination of varied terrain, natural beauty, and historical significance makes it an appealing destination for road cyclists of all levels.
tips: Considered the medieval star of the Camino de Santiago, this marvelous city is an important point on the pilgrims' journey to Santiago, and has been so for centuries. Nicknamed "La Toledo del Norte", it is a social, cultural, historical and tourist center of the Navarrese interior. Among its wide range of places to visit, the Church of San Pedro de la Rúa, the Palace of the Kings of Navarra and the Church of San Miguel stand out, all jewels of Romanesque architecture of the time. It is also fun and interesting to walk its streets with very colorful houses and a very typical architecture of this area.

Many consider her the oldest inhabitant of Navarra and that is how her figure denotes it. It is undoubtedly a specimen that has been there for several centuries, resisting the onslaught of the weather and serving as a shelter for those who need shelter along the way. Its open trunk is a clear indication of its great age, but despite this it is still standing there, firm and dominating the landscapes of Eraul.
Precious panoramic views from the road towards the reservoir and against the Alloz reservoir and the valley of the Salado river.
